objective_summary: Classify support tickets by issue type and urgency.

usable_candidates.compatibility_reason: Classification-style normalization is available via 'body' and 'priority'.

prompt.template:
Classify the following example. Return only the label.

Input:
{body}

Label:

sample_rows.subject: Wesentlicher Sicherheitsvorfall
sample_rows.body: Sehr geehrtes Support-Team,\n\nich möchte einen gravierenden Sicherheitsvorfall melden, der gegenwärtig mehrere Komponenten unserer Infrastruktur betrifft. B...
sample_rows.answer: Vielen Dank für die Meldung des kritischen Sicherheitsvorfalls und die Bereitstellung der Übersicht über die betroffenen Geräte sowie der ergriffenen ersten ...

sample_rows.subject: Account Disruption
sample_rows.body: Dear Customer Support Team,\n\nI am writing to report a significant problem with the centralized account management portal, which currently appears to be off...
sample_rows.answer: Thank you for reaching out, <name>. We are aware of the outage affecting the centralized account management system, and our technical team is actively workin...

sample_rows.subject: Query About Smart Home System Integration Features
sample_rows.body: Dear Customer Support Team,\n\nI hope this message reaches you well. I am reaching out to request detailed information about the capabilities of your smart h...
sample_rows.answer: Thank you for your inquiry. Our products support integration with Amazon Alexa, Google Assistant, and Apple HomeKit. Compatibility details can differ dependi...

warnings: License is CC-BY-NC-4.0 — restricts commercial use; verify fit with your use case.
Synthetic / generator-origin artifacts likely — validate label quality on a human-reviewed sample before wide deployment.
Class imbalance (high/critical cases are often rare) and taxonomy differences vs your production labels — plan label mapping and resampling.
Channel/format mismatch vs chat transcripts (emails vs conversations) — if you need chat-style examples consider adding a conversational dataset later.

spec.objective_summary: Classify support tickets by issue type and urgency.

warnings: License is CC-BY-NC-4.0 — restricts commercial use; verify fit with your use case.
Synthetic / generator-origin artifacts likely — validate label quality on a human-reviewed sample before wide deployment.
Class imbalance (high/critical cases are often rare) and taxonomy differences vs your production labels — plan label mapping and resampling.
Channel/format mismatch vs chat transcripts (emails vs conversations) — if you need chat-style examples consider adding a conversational dataset later.
selected_datasets.include_reason: Directly usable ticket/email dataset (61.7K rows) with deterministic classification fields ('body' -> input, 'priority' -> label). Serves as a single core SFT dataset covering issue-type and urgency without needing multi-dataset mapping.

notes: Normalization: use the dataset's provided prompt_completion classification_template (SFT-compatible) mapping 'body' -> prompt and 'priority' -> completion; keep final shape as prompt-completion.
Validate and remap label taxonomy to your production schema (issue-type buckets and urgency tiers); prepare resampling/upsampling for rare high-severity cases.
Perform PII scrubbing and sample-based label quality checks prior to full training.
Be mindful of CC-BY-NC-4.0 license restrictions for commercial deployment; retain provenance and review compliance.
If conversational/chat examples are later required, add only datasets that normalize to the same SFT prompt-completion shape to avoid mixing shapes.

validation_errors: training_params.beta must be a positive number.
